text: Becky Biberstein Michael Green Frances Carr Tom Richhart Troy Cook Bobbie Jackson Kimberly Abney Sydnee Bradburn Lean ne Lee Jeff Tillman Mike Holland Richard Payne and Matt Norris Sixth Sunday of Easter May 10 2026 This weekend May 9th 10th the Church celebrates the Sixth Sunday of Easter Our readings are both anticipating and preparing us for the coming Solemnity of the Ascension Having reflected on the divine love of the Resurrection we are now moving towards the Ascension Marking forty days after Easter this coming solemnity serves as a reminder that the Resurrection was not the end of Easter but only the beginning At the start of this joyous season we highlighted how the Easter liturgy was first and foremost baptismal in nature At the Easter Vigil our Elect passed through the waters of baptism to become members of the Body of Christ Regenerated in those saving waters they entered into the death of Christ and rose to new life with Him Just like Easter baptism is just the beginning of our life in Christ It does not end there It is directed towards heaven In the coming Ascension we will celebrate the God Man arising to the right hand of the Father and opening the gates of eternal life to all men Ten days following the Ascension we celebrate Pentecost and the sending forth of the Holy Spirit to enliven the Church the Body of Christ These mysteries of salvation lived out in the life of our Lord are now being lived out in our own lives Christ prepared the way so that we might walk it now with Him Happy Mother's Day We wish a Happy Mother's Day to all of the mothers of our parish Whether the mother of a child grandmother adoptive mother aunt godmoth er spiritual mother and all those who share in any way in the vocation of motherhood know of our love and gratitude for you In the words of our Holy Father Pope Leo May every mother feel the Church's embrace and may their sacrifices be recognized as seeds of holiness After Mass this weekend we have a special gift of carnations to give to the women of our parish as a sign of our love and gratitude for you Thank you for your presence in our lives and for how you live out in your own God given way the great gift of motherhood in our lives These carnations help support women throughout our area as a donation has been made for them to support Northeast Indiana Right to Life from our St Vincent de Paul Fund Distribution of the Most Precious Blood As you may have noticed we have paused the distribution of the Most Precious Blood at our 10 AM Sunday Masses since returning upstairs to the Church Given how tight a space we are in with our makeshift sanctuary there are no clear spaces to safely distribute the Most Precious Blood that wouldn't overly congest the area during the distribution of Holy Communion We will resume distribution of the Most Precious Blood at 10 AM Sunday Masses after our renovation project is complete As a reminder we are in need of volunteers to serve as Extraordinary Ministers of Holy Communion and Lectors for all of our weekend liturgies If you feel called to either of these ministries please contact the Parish Office for more information Baby Bottles for Helping Hands As you leave this weekend you are invited to take a Baby Bottle home with you This annual fundraiser supports Helping Hands one of our local family and pregnancy resource centers Baby Bottles can be taken home and filled with change dollar bills andor checks Please make checks payable to Helping Hands Baby Bottles can be returned in the collection basket or to the Parish Office by Father's Day June 21st in support of this good cause Graduation Mass On Sunday June 14th at the 10 AM Mass we will celebrate our annual Graduate's Mass for our graduating High School Seniors If you have a high school senior who is graduating and would like to be honored at this Mass please contact the Parish Office for more information Saturday May 9 400 4:45pm Confession 5:00pm
